description: Curated movie & clip library for Level 0 learners. Short, slow, visual content with clear speech. Each clip has a structured learning task — never passive watching.

content_rules.method: Watch with subtitles → pause & repeat → shadow 5 sentences
content_rules.frequency: 1 clip per day as part of Listening task (Task 5)
content_rules.never: Never passive entertainment. Every clip has a task attached.

clips.title: English Greetings for Beginners
clips.url_search: BBC Learning English greetings beginners
clips.task: Watch. List all greeting phrases. Repeat each 3 times.

clips.title: How to Introduce Yourself in English
clips.url_search: English with Lucy introduce yourself beginner
clips.task: Watch. Write down the structure. Record your own introduction.

clips.title: Pixar Short: 'Day & Night'
clips.url_search: Pixar short Day and Night
clips.task: Watch without sound first. Describe what happens in 3 English sentences.

clips.title: Basic English Conversation: Meeting Someone
clips.url_search: EngVid basic conversation meeting someone
clips.task: Watch. Write the dialogue. Practice with a partner or alone (both roles).

clips.title: Numbers 1-20 Song
clips.url_search: English numbers 1-20 song slow
clips.task: Listen 3 times. Sing along. Write numbers 1-20 in words.

clips.title: What's Your Name? Simple Dialogue
clips.url_search: Easy English what's your name dialogue
clips.task: Shadow the entire dialogue. Record yourself doing both roles.

clips.title: Phonics: Short Vowel Sounds
clips.url_search: Rachel's English short vowel sounds beginners
clips.task: Watch. Practice each vowel sound. Record all 5 short vowels.

clips.title: Telling Time in English
clips.url_search: BBC Learning English telling time
clips.task: Watch. Write 5 times you hear. Say what time it is NOW.

clips.title: Days of the Week Song
clips.url_search: days of the week song English slow
clips.task: Sing along 3 times. Write all 7 days without looking.

clips.title: Daily Routine Vocabulary
clips.url_search: English with Lucy daily routine vocabulary beginner
clips.task: Watch. List 10 daily activities. Say what YOU do at each time.

clips.title: Numbers 1-100: Listen and Repeat
clips.url_search: EngVid numbers 1-100 listen repeat
clips.task: Repeat every number. Then: random quiz yourself (what comes after 17? after 53?)

clips.title: Pixar Short: 'Geri's Game'
clips.url_search: Pixar short Geri's Game
clips.task: Watch. Describe the old man: what does he look like? What is he doing?

clips.title: What Time Do You Wake Up? Conversation
clips.url_search: Easy English what time conversation beginner
clips.task: Shadow the conversation. Answer all questions about YOUR schedule.

clips.title: Calendar Vocabulary: Months and Seasons
clips.url_search: Rachel's English months seasons pronunciation
clips.task: Watch. Say all 12 months. Note pronunciation differences from Arabic.

clips.title: Family Members Vocabulary
clips.url_search: English with Lucy family members vocabulary
clips.task: Watch. Draw your family tree. Label each person in English. Say 1 sentence about each.

clips.title: Describing People: Appearance
clips.url_search: BBC Learning English describing people appearance
clips.task: Watch. Describe 3 people you know using the adjectives from the video.

clips.title: Pixar Short: 'Bao'
clips.url_search: Pixar short Bao
clips.task: Watch. Describe the mother. How does she feel? (happy, sad, worried, proud) — use English.

clips.title: My Family: Simple Conversation
clips.url_search: Easy English my family conversation beginner
clips.task: Shadow the conversation. Then record YOUR version: talk about YOUR family.

clips.title: He/She: Third Person Practice
clips.url_search: EngVid he she third person present simple
clips.task: Watch. Write 5 sentences about a family member using he/she + verb-s.

clips.title: Jobs and Occupations
clips.url_search: English jobs occupations vocabulary beginner
clips.task: Watch. What does YOUR family do? Write: 'My father works as a ___.

clips.title: Adjectives for People: Kind, Funny, Smart
clips.url_search: Rachel's English adjectives people pronunciation
clips.task: Watch. Choose 5 adjectives. Use each to describe someone you know.

clips.title: Rooms of the House
clips.url_search: English with Lucy rooms house vocabulary
clips.task: Watch. Name all rooms in YOUR house in English. Describe one room in 4 sentences.

clips.title: My Morning Routine
clips.url_search: Easy English morning routine description
clips.task: Shadow. Then record YOUR morning routine using 'First, Then, After that, Finally.'

clips.title: Furniture & Objects at Home
clips.url_search: BBC Learning English furniture home vocabulary
clips.task: Watch. List 10 objects in your bedroom. Say where each is (on, under, next to).

clips.title: Pixar Short: 'La Luna'
clips.url_search: Pixar short La Luna
clips.task: Watch. Describe what happens using past tense: 'The boy went... He saw... They cleaned...'

clips.title: Prepositions of Place: In, On, Under
clips.url_search: EngVid prepositions place in on under beginner
clips.task: Watch. Look around your room. Say 5 sentences: 'The phone is on the table.'

clips.title: I Don't Like: Negative Sentences
clips.url_search: English don't doesn't negative sentences beginner
clips.task: Watch. Write 5 things you DON'T like about your house/city.

clips.title: Evening Routine Conversation
clips.url_search: Rachel's English evening routine connected speech
clips.task: Watch for connected speech. Shadow the parts about evening activities.

clips.title: Food Vocabulary: Fruits & Vegetables
clips.url_search: English with Lucy food vocabulary fruits vegetables
clips.task: Watch. Write 10 foods you eat regularly. Say each with correct pronunciation.

clips.title: At the Restaurant: Ordering Food
clips.url_search: BBC Learning English ordering food restaurant
clips.task: Watch. Write the dialogue. Practice ordering YOUR favorite meal.

clips.title: How Much Does It Cost?
clips.url_search: Easy English how much cost shopping
clips.task: Shadow the shopping conversation. Practice: 'How much is this? I'd like...'

clips.title: Pixar Short: 'Ratatouille Clip (kitchen)'
clips.url_search: Ratatouille kitchen scene simple English
clips.task: Watch. List all food/kitchen words you hear or see. Describe the scene.

clips.title: Do You Like...? Food Questions
clips.url_search: EngVid do you like food questions beginner
clips.task: Watch. Answer 10 'Do you like...?' questions about food out loud.

clips.title: Shopping Dialogue: At the Store
clips.url_search: English shopping dialogue store beginner
clips.task: Shadow both roles. Then record yourself as the customer.

clips.title: American Pronunciation: Food Words
clips.url_search: Rachel's English food words pronunciation
clips.task: Watch for: water (flap T!), vegetable (3 syllables), comfortable (3 not 4). Practice.

clips.title: Giving Directions in English
clips.url_search: BBC Learning English giving directions
clips.task: Watch. Give directions from your home to the nearest shop (in English).

clips.title: Places in the City
clips.url_search: English with Lucy places city vocabulary
clips.task: Watch. List 10 places near your home. Use 'There is/are' for each.

clips.title: Where Is the Bank? Conversation
clips.url_search: Easy English where is the bank directions
clips.task: Shadow. Practice asking for AND giving directions.

clips.title: Pixar Short: 'Lifted'
clips.url_search: Pixar short Lifted
clips.task: Watch. Describe WHERE things happen: 'The house is in a field. The ship is above...'

clips.title: Prepositions: Next to, Between, In front of
clips.url_search: EngVid prepositions next to between behind
clips.task: Watch. Describe your neighborhood using all prepositions from the video.

clips.title: There Is / There Are in Context
clips.url_search: English there is there are places
clips.task: Watch. Write 5 sentences about YOUR city using there is/are.

clips.title: Map Reading & Directions
clips.url_search: Rachel's English directions pronunciation turn left right
clips.task: Watch. Practice: turn left, turn right, go straight, on the corner. Note linking.

clips.title: Can / Can't: Talking About Abilities
clips.url_search: BBC Learning English can can't abilities
clips.task: Watch. List 5 things you CAN do and 5 you CAN'T. Say each out loud.

clips.title: Action Verbs for Beginners
clips.url_search: English with Lucy action verbs vocabulary beginner
clips.task: Watch. Act out each verb while saying it. Record 5 actions.

clips.title: Pixar Short: 'Partly Cloudy'
clips.url_search: Pixar short Partly Cloudy
clips.task: Watch. Describe the actions: 'The cloud makes... The bird carries... He can...'

clips.title: Adverbs: Fast, Slowly, Carefully
clips.url_search: Easy English adverbs fast slowly carefully
clips.task: Watch. Use 5 adverbs to describe how you do things: 'I eat quickly. I walk slowly.'

clips.title: Sports and Activities
clips.url_search: EngVid sports activities vocabulary beginner
clips.task: Watch. Say which sports you can/can't do. 'I can swim but I can't play tennis.'

clips.title: Can/Can't Pronunciation Difference
clips.url_search: Rachel's English can can't pronunciation difference
clips.task: Critical! Watch multiple times. /kæn/ (weak) vs /kænt/ (strong). Practice both.

clips.title: What Can You Do? Interview
clips.url_search: English what can you do interview beginner
clips.task: Shadow the interview. Then answer all questions about YOUR abilities.

clips.title: Feelings and Emotions Vocabulary
clips.url_search: English with Lucy feelings emotions vocabulary
clips.task: Watch. How do you feel TODAY? Use 3 emotion words from the video.

clips.title: Was / Were: Talking About the Past
clips.url_search: BBC Learning English was were past simple beginner
clips.task: Watch. Write 5 sentences about yesterday using was/were.

clips.title: Pixar Short: 'One Man Band'
clips.url_search: Pixar short One Man Band
clips.task: Watch. Describe what happened (past tense): 'The girl walked... She wanted... They played...'

clips.title: I Think / I Believe: Opinions
clips.url_search: Easy English giving opinions I think I believe beginner
clips.task: Watch. Give your opinion about 3 things: 'I think English is... I believe...'

clips.title: Regular Past Tense -ed Pronunciation
clips.url_search: Rachel's English -ed pronunciation past tense
clips.task: Critical! /t/ /d/ /ɪd/ — listen for the 3 different sounds. Practice 10 past verbs.

clips.title: What Did You Do Yesterday?
clips.url_search: EngVid what did you do yesterday past simple
clips.task: Watch. Answer the question in a 45-second recording. Use past tense verbs.

clips.title: Connected Speech: Going to → Gonna
clips.url_search: Rachel's English gonna wanna gotta connected speech
clips.task: FINAL CLIP! The gateway to natural speech. Practice all reductions. Record a natural-sounding paragraph using gonna, wanna, gotta.

usage_instructions.how_delivered: One clip per day is referenced in the daily task post (Task 5: Listening). The learner finds the clip on YouTube using the search term provided.
usage_instructions.why_not_direct_links: YouTube URLs change frequently. Search terms are more durable. The learner practices finding English content independently — a skill they'll need forever.
usage_instructions.progression: Weeks 1-4: focus on vocabulary extraction + shadowing short phrases. Weeks 5-8: focus on comprehension questions + connected speech observation.
usage_instructions.recording_tie_in: After watching, the learner records themselves repeating 3-5 key sentences. This connects immersion (input) to production (output).
